source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A tennis ball is dropped on a horizontal smooth surface. It bounces back to its original position after hitting the surface. The force on the ball during the collision is proportional to the length of compression of the ball. Which one of the following sketches describes the variation of its kinetic energy $K$ with time $t$ most appropriately? The figures are only illustrative and not to the scale.
<image_1>
extraction_notes: Options only appear as placeholders '(A)' to '(D)'; the actual sketch content is in the image.
image_1 is present as a PIL image object placeholder, not a file path or URL.

issues: metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains one image path.

image_dependency: Question explicitly asks which sketch/figure best represents K vs t, so the options are visual and cannot be resolved from text alone.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate strong reliance on the image.
Single registered image exists and registry_passed=true, so the visual asset is expected to be essential and available.
multi_step: Solving requires combining at least two physical phases: free-fall before impact and collision dynamics during compression/expansion.
The statement 'force on the ball during collision is proportional to compression' introduces an additional model that must be integrated with the bounce scenario.
The task is not simple lookup; it requires mapping physical reasoning to one of four graphical sketches.
verifiability: answer_manifest shows a choice-style answer and answer_text='(B)', giving a clear final target for exact-match checking.
There are four explicit choices, which makes final-answer verification straightforward once the correct option is established.
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true support reliable downstream validation, though correctness still depends on interpreting the image-based sketches.

risk_flags: Answer is an option label only; without the sketch image, independent reasoning verification is limited.
metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains one image path.

decision_reasons: Question text, answer, and the single required image are all present, so the core semantics needed for recovery are available.
The sample is strongly image-dependent, and the registered image exists with registry_passed=true, which supports retaining it in the candidate pool.
Initial scores are solid overall, especially for image dependency and verifiability, indicating good downstream usefulness.
The recorded issue is a metadata inconsistency rather than a missing core asset, so it does not justify downgrading to reject.

asset_records.normalized_text_snapshot: After the ball leaves the surface and rises back to its original position, how does its kinetic energy K vary with time t?

conditions.text: It bounces back to its original position after hitting the surface.

targets.text: Which one of the following sketches describes the variation of its kinetic energy $K$ with time $t$ most appropriately?

answer_slots.target_text: Before the tennis ball first touches the surface, how does its kinetic energy K vary with time t?
answer_slots.expected_answer: It starts from zero and increases quadratically with time (K proportional to t^2) until the ball first touches the surface.

answer_slots.target_text: During the time the ball is in contact with the surface, how does its kinetic energy change from first contact to maximum compression and then to the instant it leaves the surface?
answer_slots.expected_answer: It increases slightly just after first contact, then decreases to zero at maximum compression, and then increases again to the same value by the time the ball leaves the surface.

answer_slots.target_text: After the ball leaves the surface and rises back to its original position, how does its kinetic energy K vary with time t?
answer_slots.expected_answer: It decreases quadratically with time and becomes zero again when the ball reaches its original position.

rewrite_reports.rationale: The original correct answer is only a sketch label "(B)", so the option text does not carry usable semantic content. The stem itself is sufficient to recover the intended kinetic-energy-vs-time behavior, so the graph choice is rewritten into explicit, answerable subquestions about each phase of the motion.

variants.title: Before first contact
variants.rewritten_question_text: Before the tennis ball first touches the surface, how does its kinetic energy K vary with time t?
variants.expected_answer: It starts from zero and increases quadratically with time (K proportional to t^2) until the ball first touches the surface.

variants.title: During collision
variants.rewritten_question_text: During the time the ball is in contact with the surface, how does its kinetic energy change from first contact to maximum compression and then to the instant it leaves the surface?
variants.expected_answer: It increases slightly just after first contact, then decreases to zero at maximum compression, and then increases again to the same value by the time the ball leaves the surface.

variants.title: After rebound
variants.rewritten_question_text: After the ball leaves the surface and rises back to its original position, how does its kinetic energy K vary with time t?
variants.expected_answer: It decreases quadratically with time and becomes zero again when the ball reaches its original position.
